_purpose: Courthouse Atlas for Rock County, WI. Record survival is good — no major courthouse fires or war damage. Records continuous from county formation (1839). The county's dual agricultural-industrial character means records span farm deeds and factory employment.

previous_courthouses.fate: Replaced as the county grew. No significant fire loss.
Limestone Romanesque building. Records transferred to current building.

record_losses.cause: None — no significant fire or war damage
record_losses.description: Rock County has experienced no major courthouse fire or record loss. Records run continuously from the county's 1839 formation. Genealogical records are generally intact and accessible.
record_losses.narrative_impact: If {ancestor_name} lived in Rock County after 1839, the records very likely survive. Any gaps in the paper trail point to the ancestor's circumstances — factory workers who rented housing left fewer county records than landowners.
record_losses.source: Wisconsin Historical Society. Guide to Wisconsin County Records.

deeds.repository: Rock County Register of Deeds; Wisconsin Historical Society microfilm

wills_estates.gaps: Minor gaps in earliest loose papers
wills_estates.repository: Rock County Probate Court; Wisconsin Historical Society

marriages.completeness: Good — territorial and statehood records both survive
marriages.repository: Rock County Register of Deeds; Wisconsin Historical Society

court_minutes.repository: Rock County Clerk of Courts; Wisconsin Historical Society

vital_records.completeness: Statewide registration from 1907. Pre-1907 rely on church records and census.
vital_records.repository: Wisconsin Department of Health Services; Janesville city records may predate 1907

substitute_sources.source_type: General Motors / Parker Pen company records
substitute_sources.description: GM's Janesville plant (1919-2009) and Parker Pen Company maintained employee records that documented thousands of Rock County workers. Whether these records survive and where they are held varies by company.
substitute_sources.repository: Rock County Historical Society; Hedberg Public Library (Janesville); company archives where accessible
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Employment records can establish dates of residence, wages, and family members where county records are sparse — particularly for factory workers who rented rather than owned homes.
substitute_sources.source: Goldstein, Amy. Janesville: An American Story. Simon & Schuster, 2017.

substitute_sources.source_type: Church records — Norwegian Lutheran and Congregational
substitute_sources.description: Norwegian Lutheran and New England Congregational churches were the primary denominations. Both maintained records of baptisms, marriages, and burials.
substitute_sources.repository: Individual congregations; Wisconsin Historical Society; ELCA archives; Norwegian-American Historical Association
substitute_sources.genealogical_value: Church records bridge the gap before statewide vital registration in 1907.
substitute_sources.source: Wisconsin Historical Society church records guide.

narrative_hooks: Rock County's records survive intact from 1839 — if {ancestor_name} owned land, married, or appeared in court, the documentation is there.
If {ancestor_name} worked at the GM plant or Parker Pen in Janesville, company records may document what the county records do not — daily work life in an industrial economy.
Wisconsin vital registration began in 1907 — before that, {ancestor_name}'s birth or death must be traced through church records, census, or the Janesville newspaper.

Rock County sits on the glacial boundary — {ancestor_name}'s farm on the flat eastern prairie left different records than a farm in the hilly Driftless west, because flat land was easier to survey, easier to deed, and easier to subdivide.
source_references: Wisconsin Historical Society. Guide to Wisconsin County Records. Madison: WHS, various editions.
Goldstein, Amy. Janesville: An American Story. New York: Simon & Schuster, 2017.
Wisconsin Historical Society church records guide.
